strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] analyse key similarities and differences in social values and aspects of life between people in present-day Canada and some different groups and communities, including First Nations, Métis, and Inuit communities, in Canada between 1713 and 1800

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] analyse some of the main challenges facing various individuals, groups, and/or communities, including First Nations, Métis, and Inuit individuals and/or communities, in Canada between 1713 and 1800 and ways in which people responded to those challenges

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] analyse the displacement experienced by various groups and communities, including First Nations, Métis, and Inuit communities, who were living in or who came to Canada between 1713 and 1800

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] formulate questions to guide investigations into perspectives of different groups and communities, including First Nations, Métis, and/or Inuit communities, on some significant events, developments, and/or issues related to the shift in power in colonial Canada from France to Britain

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] gather and organize information and evidence about perspectives of different groups and communities, including First Nations, Métis, and/or Inuit communities, on some significant events, developments, and/or issues related to the shift in power in colonial Canada from France to Britain, using a variety of primary sources

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] assess the credibility of sources and information relevant to their investigations

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] analyse and construct maps as part of their investigations into significant events, developments, and/or issues related to the shift in power in colonial Canada from France to Britain, with a focus on exploring their spatial boundaries

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] interpret and analyse information and evidence relevant to their investigations, using a variety of tools

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] evaluate evidence and draw conclusions about perspectives of different groups and communities, including First Nation, Métis, and/or Inuit communities, on some significant events, developments, or issues related to the shift in power in colonial Canada from France to Britain

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] communicate the results of their inquiries using appropriate vocabulary

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] identify factors leading to some key events that occurred in and/or affected Canada between 1713 and 1800

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] identify a few key treaties of relevance to Indigenous people during this period, including wampum belts exchanged, and explain the significance of some of these agreements for different people and communities in Canada

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] identify key political and legal changes that occurred in and/or affected Canada during this period

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] identify key social and economic changes that occurred in and/or affected Canada during this period

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] describe some significant aspects of daily life in various First Nations, Métis, and Inuit communities in Canada during this period

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] describe some significant aspects of daily life of different newcomer/settler groups living in Canada during this period

strand_name: New France and British North America, 1713-1800
desc: [History] describe significant interactions between various individuals, groups, and institutions in Canada during this period
